name: components/list
description: Styled lists with CSS-drawn markers (check, arrow, numbered, timeline): the sanctioned form for short items, replacing grids of one-line boxes.

usage: Use a <ul> or <ol> with the class on the list element; markers are drawn in CSS, so pick exactly one marker modifier and never add emoji or glyph bullets.
Single-line items go straight in the <li>; two-line items use ls-list__title plus ls-list__text.
Prefer a list over a grid of one-line boxes: short items do not earn borders.

composition.itemCountGuidance: 3-6 items; split longer lists across slides or columns.
composition.copyGuidance: Single-line items up to 10 words; titled items pair a title of up to 5 words with one sentence of text.
useWhen: 3-6 short parallel points need a marker and rhythm
an ordered sequence of takeaways suits numbered markers
a compact chronology suits the timeline rail
avoidWhen: each item needs multiple sentences of explanation
the sequence describes a process whose steps deserve markers and connectors

useCases: Present 3-6 short takeaways with CSS-drawn markers.
Show a numbered sequence of decisions or milestones.
Render a compact chronology on a timeline rail.
